questions.q: The pig ran off into the dark and Grunk can't see a trail. Which way did it go?
hints: Grunk's eyes aren't much use out here in the dark — but Grunk has other senses.
Try using your ears, not your eyes.
LOOK FOR PIG, then LISTEN. The direction to go will become obvious after that.

questions.q: Grunk's torch went out when he fell. Did he do something wrong?
hints: No — that was just the fall. Nothing to fix yet, and nothing lost by exploring in the dark for now.
Pick up what's down here with you before moving on.

questions.q: What's this metal tube for?
hints: Grunk should find out what's inside it before deciding it's useless.
Try blowing into it.
BLOW IN TUBE. It's a whistle.

questions.q: The pig keeps running away whenever Grunk gets close.
hints: Chasing it bare-handed isn't going to work — the pig is faster than Grunk.
Pigs care about one thing more than running away: food. Grunk needs something the pig wants.
Don't worry about the pig yet. Come back once Grunk has found something worth tempting it with.

questions.q: Is there anything worth Grunk's attention in the Fountain Room?
hints: Look closely at everything in here — the bowl, the statue, the walls.
The fountain's bowl holds something small and shiny that Grunk shouldn't leave behind.
X BOWL — Grunk spots the coin inside and pockets it on his own. Also X STATUE, then TAKE HAT and WEAR HAT.

questions.q: There are two carved murals in the Statue Room. Do they mean anything?
hints: Grunk should study both walls closely — they're not just decoration.
One shows fire and water together. Water usually puts fire out — but is that really what this picture is showing?
Remember what's on these murals. The west one (fire and a bucket) and the east one (a pole and floating things) both describe things Grunk will need to do later.

questions.q: The statue is posed strangely — one hand out, one hand raised. Does that matter?
hints: A statue posed that deliberately is probably a clue, not just art.
One hand points toward a direction Grunk hasn't been able to go yet. The other hand looks like it's meant to be holding something.
Remember this pose. Later, once Grunk has a lit torch again, come back and try PUT TORCH IN HAND.

questions.q: What is this box with a coin slot and a lever for?
hints: It looks like it's built to be operated, not just looked at.
Put the coin in the slot, then see what the lever does.
PUT COIN IN SLOT, PULL LEVER — a brick drops into the basket below.

questions.q: Grunk used his coin in the box. Now what? Does he need to find more coins?
hints: Look closely at the box itself — is there anything odd about it?
There's a dent in the box. Dents like that usually mean 'hit here.'
HIT BOX and the coin falls back out into the basket — Grunk can put it in and pull the lever again as many times as he needs.

questions.q: Is this chair worth carrying around?
hints: Grunk should hang onto it — it may come in handy somewhere he can't quite reach something.
TAKE CHAIR and bring it along; there's a shelf elsewhere too high to reach on foot.

questions.q: There's someone asleep in here, but nothing Grunk does gets a response.
hints: Looking and listening quietly isn't going to rouse anyone.
Grunk needs to make some real noise.
SHOUT. That should wake him up — then GREET GNOME to start talking.

questions.q: The gnome is awake and talking, but Grunk isn't sure what to ask him.
hints: The gnome tells Grunk exactly what he can try next, right after most things he says — look for the bracketed suggestions in his dialogue.
Try greeting him, telling him about Grunk, and asking about the things Grunk has already seen — the statue, for instance.
Follow the bracketed [Grunk can try ...] suggestions as they appear; they'll walk Grunk through the whole conversation naturally.

questions.q: Is there anything else worth checking in here?
hints: Grunk should look in more places than just what's in plain sight.
LOOK UNDER BED, and also OPEN TRUNK — there's something inside worth taking.

questions.q: There's something on the top shelf, but Grunk can't reach it.
hints: Grunk needs to get higher up somehow.
Did Grunk bring anything along that he could stand on?
DROP CHAIR, then STAND ON CHAIR, then TAKE BOOK.

questions.q: Grunk found a book missing a page. What should he do with it?
hints: The gnome seemed like the kind of person who'd care about a book.
Bring it back to him, and ask about the page while you're at it.
GIVE BOOK TO GNOME, then ASK GNOME ABOUT PAGE. He can't fix the book yet — the missing page itself is somewhere else in the caves.

questions.q: There's a chest in here too. Worth opening?
hints: Worth a try — but the chest has other ideas.
It's locked, and Grunk has no key yet — one will turn up as Grunk explores. Meanwhile, TAKE POLE — the long green pole leaning by the doorway.

questions.q: There's a small red something on the far side of the stream, out of Grunk's reach. Worth investigating?
hints: Grunk can't reach it by hand — something long might.
Poke it with the pole from the Shelf Room and see what happens.
TOUCH THING WITH POLE — the red thing sticks to the pole, and it turns out to be a key. While Grunk is here: FILL HAT WITH WATER.

questions.q: There's a locked chest in here. Grunk found a key somewhere — does it fit?
hints: If Grunk has fished a key out of the stream, that's the one. If not, the stream cave deserves a closer look first.
Unlock the chest and look inside — a heap of black powder.

UNLOCK CHEST, OPEN CHEST. Then remember the west mural in the Statue Room — fire and a bucket of water — and read it backwards: POUR WATER ON POWDER (the water Grunk scooped up in his hat) makes it ignite, not douse. Then LIGHT TORCH WITH FIRE.

questions.q: There's a piece of paper stuck down in that crack Grunk noticed earlier, but he can't quite grab it.
hints: Grunk's fingers aren't going to reach it. He needs a tool.
The pole might reach, but plain wood won't grip the paper.
The pole needs to be sticky, not just long. BURN POLE WITH TORCH first, then TAKE PAPER WITH POLE.

sections.title: Delivering the Page, Catching the Pig

questions.q: Grunk has the missing page now. What next?
hints: Bring it to the one person who's been missing it.
Hand it over — and don't wander off right away.
GIVE PAPER TO GNOME, then WAIT. He'll get to work fixing the book on his own.

questions.q: Grunk has bricks now. How does he actually catch the pig with them?
hints: Showing the pig the bricks is a start, but the pig needs a reason to stop running.
The pig cares about eating, not about being chased. Give it something to focus on, then be patient.
SHOW BRICK TO PIG, DROP ALL BRICKS, then wait several turns while the pig eats. Once it's absorbed in the bricks instead of watching Grunk, TAKE PIG.

sections.title: Statue Room — Opening the North Passage

questions.q: That statue's raised hand looks like it's meant to hold something. What?
hints: Remember its pose — one hand pointing, one hand raised, empty.
The lit torch would fit right in that raised hand.
PUT TORCH IN HAND. That opens a passage to the north.

questions.q: Grunk needs to get through the windy maze up ahead, but the torch alone won't cut it.
hints: The gnome had a glowing ball earlier — that could serve as a light source too.
Go retrieve the torch from the statue's hand first, then see if the gnome will trade.
TAKE TORCH, then bring it to the gnome: ASK GNOME FOR BALL, GIVE TORCH TO GNOME.

questions.q: Grunk is completely lost in these twisty tunnels. Mapping isn't working. What now?
hints: This place isn't meant to be solved by mapping it out.
Grunk has something that makes noise, from way back at the very start.
PLAY WHISTLE. The gnome will hear it and come find Grunk, then guide him out — just FOLLOW GNOME from there.

questions.q: Is it possible to get every point in this story?
hints: The game itself will tell you once you've finished the main quest — look at the MENU option offered at the ending screen.
The last point is an optional bit of silliness outside the main quest, and the game's own end-of-game MENU spells out exactly how to get it — no need to hunt for it beforehand.

sources.name: Lost Pig walkthrough (author-provided competition walkthrough, IF Archive)
